vocabulario.traduccion: cortarse el pelo (te lo hace otra persona)

banco_oraciones.oracion: Sofía had her website redesigned last month.
Ben gets his hair cut every three weeks.
We're having the office painted this weekend.
They had their photos taken by a professional.
Sofía didn't have her logo designed by an agency; she did it herself.
Ben doesn't get his shoes repaired, he just buys new ones.
We haven't had the car serviced this year.
Where did you have your website designed?
Do you get your teeth checked every year?
Have they had the roof fixed yet?
Did Sofía have her website redesigned? Yes, she did.
Does Ben get his hair cut often? Yes, he does.
Have you had your car serviced this year? No, I haven't.

interaccion: Kenji: Your new website looks amazing. Did you build it yourself?
Sofía: No, I **had it designed** by a friend — I just gave her the ideas.
Kenji: Smart. I always **get my photos edited** by someone else too, I have no eye for it.
Sofía: Exactly. You don't have to do everything yourself — just **have it done** well.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: I designed my logo by a professional.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: I had my logo designed by a professional.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Use have/get something done when someone else does a job for you — the subject arranges it, but doesn't do the action.

ejercicios.pregunta: Sofía ___ her website redesigned last month.
Ben gets his hair ___ every three weeks.
We're having the office ___ this weekend.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'I had my logo designed' means I designed it myself.
Causative 'have/get something done' uses the past participle of the verb.
'Get' is more informal than 'have' in this structure.

ejercicios.texto: Sofía had her website ___ (redesign) last month.
We're getting the office ___ (paint) this weekend.
They had their photos ___ (take) by a professional.

ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía had her logo designed by a friend
Ben gets his hair cut every month

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I designed my logo by a professional.
ejercicios.respuesta: I had my logo designed by a professional.

ejercicios.instruccion: Rewrite using the causative (Sofía as subject).
ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía had her logo designed by a friend.

ejercicios.oracion_base: Did Sofía have her website redesigned?
Sofía had her logo designed by an agency.
ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía didn't have her logo designed by an agency.

ejercicios.pregunta: Do you get your hair cut often? (short answer, Yes)

guion: Kenji: Your website looks so professional now.
Sofía: Thanks, I had it redesigned by a friend from design school.
Kenji: I need to get my resume looked at by someone too. I always mess up the formatting.
Sofía: I know someone who does that. She helped me get my portfolio organized last year.
Kenji: Perfect. I'll have her check it before I apply anywhere.

comprension.pregunta: What does Kenji want help with?

reading.texto: When Sofía started her studio, she tried to do everything herself — design, invoicing, even her own website. Three years later, she's learned to delegate. She had her website redesigned by a friend from design school, gets her invoices handled by an accountant twice a month, and even has her social media managed by an intern on weekends. 'I used to think doing it all myself made me a real professional,' she told Kenji. 'Now I know a real professional knows when to have something done by someone better at it.'

preguntas.afirmacion: Sofía designed her own website when her studio started.
An accountant now handles her invoices.
Sofía manages her own social media every day.
Sofía believes real professionals never delegate.
